verdict_reasoning: ABCB9/TAPL is a multi-pass ABC transporter resident exclusively in the lysosomal limiting membrane. Its TMD0 encodes the lysosomal targeting signal; full-length TAPL traffics ER→Golgi→endosomes→lysosomes and is explicitly documented as not found on the plasma membrane. Pre-no checklist: (1) no cell-surface-directed therapeutic program; (2) not a shed TM-precursor proligand — it is a multi-pass integral lysosomal TM protein; (3) no covalent/wash-resistant TM-partner tethering at the outer leaflet; (4) no documented physiological PM minority pool or cycling — the only PM-localized form is an artificial TMD0-truncated core construct, not the native protein; (5) no gene-specific evidence for cancer/oncogenic ecto-display, cell-death-induced outer-leaflet exposure, germline/developmental-restricted PM display, activation-induced surfacing, or lysosomal-exocytosis-mediated PM delivery of this transporter.
